content.problemStatement:
Chefland can be represented as a NNN by MMM grid. The rows of the grid are numbered from 111 to NNN, and the columns of the grid are numbered from 111 to MMM. The square in row XXX and column YYY of the grid can be uniquely identified as square (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y). Each grid square has a cost of either 000 or 111.

Chef is going on a vacation to Chefland for the next QQQ days. On the ithi^{th}ith of these days, Chef would like to travel from square (Ai,Bi)(A_i, B_i)(Ai​,Bi​) to square (Ci,Di)(C_i, D_i)(Ci​,Di​). Here, it is guaranteed that both Ai≤CiA_i \leq C_iAi​≤Ci​ and Bi≤DiB_i \leq D_iBi​≤Di​. Due to restrictions on movement in Chefland, it is only possible to travel downwards or to the right. In other words, if Chef is in a square (X,Y)(X, Y)(X,Y), he is only able to move to square (X,Y+1)(X, Y+1)(X,Y+1) or (X+1,Y)(X+1, Y)(X+1,Y) in one move. The cost of Chef's trip is defined as the product of the costs of the grid squares he passes through on his journey from the starting square to the ending square. Note that this product also includes the costs of the starting square and the ending square.

For each of the QQQ days, find the minimum cost Chef would need to pay to travel between the starting and the ending squares.


Input Format
The first line of input contains two space-separated integers, NNN and MMM. NNN represents the number of rows in Chefland, while MMM represents the number of columns.


The following NNN lines each contain MMM space-separated integers, each of which is 000 or 111. On the ithi^{th}ith line, the jthj^{th}jth integer represents the cost of the grid square numbered (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) in the grid.


The following line contains QQQ, the number of days Chef will travel.


The ithi^{th}ith of the following QQQ lines each contains four space-separated integers, AiA_iAi​, BiB_iBi​, CiC_iCi​ and DiD_iDi​, in order.



Output Format
For each of the QQQ trips, output the minimum possible cost of the trip on a new line.
constraints: Each cell value is either 0 or 1
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two integers N and M – the number of rows and columns of the grid. The next N lines each contain M integers (0 or 1) describing the cost of each cell. The following line contains an integer Q – the number of queries. Each of the next Q lines contains four integers A B C D (1‑based) with A ≤ C and B ≤ D, describing a trip from (A,B) to (C,D).
content.outputFormat: For each query output a single integer on its own line – the minimum possible product cost of a valid path (0 or 1).

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each query we check whether the rectangle defined by the start and end contains a zero. If it does, the minimum product is 0, otherwise it is 1. The computed answers match the sample output.
The only cell is 0, therefore any path (the single cell) has product 0.
All cells are 1, so the product of any path is 1.

solution.approach: Because cell values are only 0 or 1, the product of a path is 0 if the path contains at least one zero, otherwise it is 1. Any down‑right path from (A,B) to (C,D) stays inside the axis‑aligned rectangle [(A,B),(C,D)]. Therefore the answer is 0 iff the rectangle contains at least one cell with value 0. Pre‑compute a 2‑D prefix sum of the number of zeros. For each query, retrieve the number of zeros in O(1) using the inclusion‑exclusion formula. If the count > 0 output 0, else output 1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·M + Q) – building the prefix sum scans the whole grid once, each query is O(1).

code.Python3:
import sys
def main():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    N = int(next(it))
    M = int(next(it))
    pref = [[0]*(M+1) for _ in range(N+1)]
    for i in range(1, N+1):
        for j in range(1, M+1):
            v = int(next(it))
            zero = 1 if v==0 else 0
            pref[i][j] = pref[i-1][j] + pref[i][j-1] - pref[i-1][j-1] + zero
    Q = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(Q):
        A = int(next(it)); B = int(next(it)); C = int(next(it)); D = int(next(it))
        zeros = pref[C][D] - pref[A-1][D] - pref[C][B-1] + pref[A-1][B-1]
        out.append('0' if zeros else '1')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
